title: Why We Built Lumivex — A Letter from Our Founders
excerpt: Two engineers walk into a hospital waiting room. That's not a joke — it's how Lumivex started.
author: Dr. Anika Sharma & James Whitfield
content:
It started in a hospital waiting room in Boston, 2020. James was there for his annual physical. Dr. Sharma — Anika — was visiting her father, who had just been admitted for AFib they caught too late. They didn't know each other yet.

James was wearing the most expensive smartwatch on the market. He'd just finished a 5K that morning. His watch said he was in peak form. The hospital's 12-lead ECG said otherwise: an arrhythmia was hiding in plain sight, invisible to the optical sensor on his wrist.

Anika saw the watch. She saw the hospital monitor. She saw the gap.

"That's when I realized the best consumer wearable on Earth couldn't detect what a $200 clinical ECG catches in five seconds," Anika recalls. "The technology existed. Nobody had put it on a wrist properly."

Six months later, they had a prototype. It was ugly — a breadboard strapped to a wristband with medical tape. But it worked. The dry-electrode single-lead ECG they designed caught AFib with 94% sensitivity on their first 50 test subjects. The algorithm got better every week.

Lumivex was born from a simple conviction: your watch should know your body as well as your doctor does. Maybe better — because your watch is with you 24/7, and your doctor sees you twice a year.

What We Believe

Health intelligence is not a luxury. It's not something you should need an $800 budget and a medical degree to access. Core starts at $179 because that belief is not marketing copy — it's why we exist.

We don't make gadgets. We make guardians — devices that watch over the people who wear them, that catch what human eyes miss, that turn raw biometrics into insights you can actually use.

200,000 people now wear Lumivex. That's 200,000 people who know their bodies better than they did yesterday. We're just getting started.
metaTitle: Why We Built Lumivex — Founders' Letter | Lumivex Blog
metaDescription: The story behind Lumivex: two engineers, a hospital waiting room, and a conviction that health intelligence shouldn't cost $800.

faq.question: Where is Lumivex based?
faq.answer: We're headquartered in Boston, MA, with an engineering office in Amsterdam for EU operations.

faq.question: Are you hiring?
faq.answer: Always. Check our careers page or email talent@lumivex.com — we're especially looking for embedded systems engineers and ML researchers.
